sections.heading: Use elevation to define the layering between elements in a design when required. Flat is the style we primarily follow. But considering elevation will be needed when many elements come together.
Interaction (Tooltip & max width)
content: Hovering over tags brings up a tooltip with the full tag content.
Tags truncate automatically when they exceed the maximum width.

sections.heading: Layout
content: Tags are laid out horizontally to fill containers. Make sure there is at least 4px between tags.
Tags will reflow onto the next line when container size is limited.

sections.heading: Usage
content:
Elevation MUST be used only to define the layering between elements in a design when required. The purpose of having elevation remains only this and not merely adding visual aesthetics. 

Only use drop shadows, colors and/or overlays to depict elevation. Do not use bevels, borders, opacity, etc. to depict layering or elevation. 

Buttons may be considered elevated from their surface depending on situations. 

Elevation MUST be depicted in either of these 3 ways:  
Drop shadows (recommended)  
Color difference (when obvious) 
Overlays (when required to separate from whole underlying layers).
